steps.title: Log in to Netflix
steps.description: Go to Netflix.com and sign in with your account.

steps.title: Access Account Settings
steps.description: Click your profile icon → Account.

steps.title: Cancel Membership
steps.description: Under 'Membership & billing', click 'Cancel membership'.
steps.warning: You can continue watching until the end of the billing period. No refund for prepaid months.

steps.title: Confirm Cancellation
steps.description: Follow the prompts. Netflix will send a confirmation email.
steps.tip: Save the email; if you're charged later, you have proof.

general_tips: Cancel at least 1 day before renewal to avoid next cycle charge
If you have a gift card balance, you may lose it upon cancellation
Download your watchlist before cancellation if you want to keep it

steps.title: Log in to Spotify
steps.description: Visit spotify.com and log in.

steps.title: Go to Subscription Page
steps.description: Click 'Account' → 'Subscription'.

steps.title: Cancel Premium
steps.description: Click 'Change or cancel' → 'Cancel Premium'.
steps.warning: You'll revert to Free (ad-supported) tier immediately. No refunds for partial months.

steps.title: Confirm via Email
steps.description: Spotify will email a cancellation confirmation.
steps.tip: Check spam folder if not received within 24h.

general_tips: Cancellation is effective immediately; you won't be charged next cycle
Your playlists remain, but offline downloads are removed
You can resubscribe anytime without losing data

steps.title: Access AWS Billing Console
steps.description: Go to AWS Console → Billing & Cost Management.

steps.title: Cancel Individual Services
steps.description: AWS doesn't have a single subscription. Cancel each service (EC2, RDS, S3, etc.) individually.
steps.warning: Simply deleting resources isn't enough; you must cancel the service subscription where applicable.

steps.title: Set Billing Alarm
steps.description: Before leaving, set up a billing alarm to monitor unexpected charges.
steps.tip: Highly recommended to avoid surprise costs after cancellation.

steps.title: Close Account (Optional)
steps.description: To close entire AWS account: Contact AWS Support with 'account closure' request.
steps.warning: All data will be permanently deleted. Use with caution.

general_tips: AWS charges are pay-as-you-go; you may not have recurring subscriptions, just running resources
Monitor the 'Billing' dashboard for a month before assuming you're safe
Delete all resources: EC2, EBS, RDS, Lambda, etc.

steps.title: Log in to Stripe Dashboard
steps.description: Go to dashboard.stripe.com and authenticate.

steps.title: Check Active Subscriptions
steps.description: Navigate to Billing → Subscriptions. See if any are active.

steps.title: Cancel Subscriptions
steps.description: For each subscription, click '...' → Cancel subscription.
steps.warning: Canceling a subscription stops future invoices but does not refund past payments.

steps.title: Delete API Keys (Optional)
steps.description: If no longer using Stripe, revoke all API keys in Developers → API keys.
steps.tip: Revoke both live and test keys if not needed.

general_tips: Stripe is a payment processor; you likely have subscriptions with other services using Stripe, not Stripe itself
To cancel services billed via Stripe, contact the merchant directly; Stripe cannot cancel on your behalf
Disputing past charges with your bank is possible but may affect relationships

steps.title: Log in to PayPal
steps.description: Go to paypal.com and sign in.

steps.title: Go to Pre-approved Payments
steps.description: Click Settings (gear) → Payments → Manage pre-approved payments.

steps.title: Cancel Recurring Payments
steps.description: Find the merchant and click 'Cancel' next to each.
steps.warning: Do NOT cancel by contacting PayPal (slow). Use the 'Cancel' button in pre-approved payments.

steps.title: Confirm Cancellation
steps.description: PayPal will send email confirmation. Keep it.
steps.tip: Also contact the merchant directly to confirm they won't charge you again.

general_tips: Pre-approved payments are hard to cancel if merchant doesn't cooperate; PayPal may not help
Consider blocking the merchant via 'Dispute' if unauthorized
Regularly review this list; unauthorized subscriptions are common

steps.title: Go to GitHub Billing
steps.description: Visit github.com/settings/billing while logged in.

steps.title: Manage Plans
steps.description: Under 'Plans', see current GitHub Pro, Team, or Enterprise plan.

steps.title: Downgrade or Cancel
steps.description: Click 'Change plan' → select 'Free' or 'Cancel plan'.
steps.warning: Downgrading to Free immediately removes private repos unless you have Pro. Back up first.

steps.title: Check for Add-ons
steps.description: Also check 'Features' and 'Add-ons' sections for separate charges (e.g., Codespaces).

general_tips: GitHub charges monthly; cancel at any time, no prorated refunds typically
If part of an organization, you may be on a team plan managed by org owner
